offensive.name: Blood Price
offensive.description: Trade your suffering for overwhelming force — spending hit dice while Wounded to maximize damage and eventually entering Pain Ascendance where nothing can reduce you to zero.
tiers: When you cast an effect while Wounded, you may expend hit dice equal to your proficiency bonus — add the total rolled as Sanguine damage to one damage roll.
While you have 1 or more Injuries, you gain a bonus to one spell save DC of your choice equal to half your proficiency bonus.
You may voluntarily gain 1d4 Wounds as part of casting. If you do, your next attack or effect deals maximum damage to one target.

Once per long rest, if you have at least 1 Injury, enter Pain Ascendance for 1 minute — you gain resistance to all damage, cast spells without material components, reroll 1s on all damage dice, and cannot be reduced to 0 HP by anything other than a critical hit.

supportive.name: Agony Echo
supportive.description: The more they hurt you, the more dangerous you become — reflecting damage taken back at attackers and eventually triggering a Chain Reaction that Wounds, Exhausts, and Staggers everything around you.
tiers: When you take damage, you may use your reaction to target one creature within 30 feet — it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Verum DC or take half the damage you took as Sanguine damage.
Target up to two creatures within 60 feet. Creatures that fail their save take the full damage you took as Sanguine damage.
Once per round you may use Agony Echo without expending your reaction. Creatures that fail their save also gain 1 level of Exhaustion.

Agony Echo becomes a Chain Reaction — when you take damage, choose up to three creatures within 90 feet. On a failed save each takes the full damage (Sanguine), gains 1 Wound, suffers 1 level of Exhaustion, and becomes Staggered (disadvantage on attacks and saves until the end of their next turn).

supportive.name: Suffering Sustains
supportive.description: You become sharpest while bleeding — gaining AC bonuses and damage while Wounded, regenerating while near death, and eventually triggering a Legendary Pain Response that grants an extra action at 1 HP.
tiers: While you have 1 or more Wounds, gain advantage on Constitution saving throws and +1 to AC.
While Wounded or Injured, you deal +2 damage on all attacks and spells and cannot be Frightened.
While at half HP or below and Wounded, gain regeneration 5 at the start of your turn. This regeneration is suppressed for 1 round if you take radiant damage.

Gain a Legendary Pain Response — when you would drop to 0 HP, remain conscious at 1 HP. While in this state you may take 1 extra action per turn, are immune to pain-induced Exhaustion, Fear, and Charm, and the state lasts 1 minute or until you are healed above half HP.

control.name: Torturer's Focus
control.description: Your pain clarifies — your enemy's blinds, stripping their resistances, stacking save penalties with every Wound, and eventually making Injuries permanent unless healed by the strongest magic.
tiers: Targets damaged by the effect must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or suffer disadvantage on their next attack roll.
Creatures that fail also lose resistance to psychic, Sanguine, and necrotic damage until the end of your next turn.
If the effect causes a Wound, the target suffers a stacking −1 penalty to saving throws for the rest of the encounter. This stacks with each Wound inflicted.

Designate one target — until the end of your next turn it automatically fails death saving throws if unconscious, cannot recover from Wounds, and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Verum DC or have any Injury suffered become permanent unless healed by Greater Restoration or stronger.

control.name: Crippling Wound
control.description: Your spell rends more than flesh — inflicting Wounds that cripple movement or grip, stacking into Injuries, and eventually destroying named body parts with permanent consequences.
tiers: If a creature fails its save, it suffers 1 Wound in addition to any damage dealt. Choose one limb or organ affected by the wound.
The Wound becomes crippling — if physical, the target suffers −10 feet of movement, disadvantage on Dexterity checks, or drops one held item (your choice).
If a creature takes 2 or more Wounds from the same effect, they must succeed on a Constitution save or gain 1 Injury from the appropriate table.

Declare a named body part when casting (heart, lungs, eyes, spine, etc.). If the creature fails the save, it suffers a thematic permanent Injury in that location unless restored by 7th level vitaemancy or divine intervention.

complementEffects.name: Nerve Strike
complementEffects.description: The spell finds the places that hurt most — the nerve response briefly overriding the target's ability to attack cleanly or hold Concentration.
complementEffects.effect: On a hit or failed saving throw against the primary effect, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Verum DC or have disadvantage on their next attack roll and their next Constitution saving throw.
upgrades: On a failed save, the disadvantage extends to all attack rolls and Constitution saves until the end of their next turn rather than just the first.

Creatures that fail also cannot maintain Concentration — they must immediately make a Concentration check against your Verum DC or lose whatever they were concentrating on, the nerve strike scattering their focus completely.

complementEffects.name: Martyr's Guard
complementEffects.description: Wounds become walls — an ally's experience of pain sharpening their instincts into a bonus to AC and saving throws equal to their current Wound count.
complementEffects.effect: When the primary effect resolves, one ally of your choice within 30 feet gains a bonus to AC and saving throws equal to their current Wound count (minimum +1) until the start of your next turn.
upgrades: The bonus persists for 1 minute rather than until your next turn, and the ally also gains advantage on death saving throws while the effect is active.
You may choose two allies to receive this benefit, and each also gains resistance to the damage type that last wounded them — their body having adapted to the pain of that specific harm.

complementEffects.name: Stagger
complementEffects.description: Too much at once — the pain signal flooding the target's nervous system strips their reaction and halves their speed until they can think past the noise.
complementEffects.effect: When the primary effect hits or a target fails their saving throw, that target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Verum DC or be Staggered — they lose their reaction and their movement speed is halved until the end of their next turn.
upgrades: On a failed save, the target also has disadvantage on the first saving throw they make before the end of their next turn — the stagger leaving them slow to respond to the next threat.
Creatures that fail also cannot take bonus actions until the end of their next turn, the stagger consuming every spare moment of their attention.
